heading: Qualified liquidations of LIFO inventories
label: 473 Qualified liquidations of LIFO inventories
notes: Effective Date, , , as amended by , , , provided that:Pub. L. 96–223, title IV, § 403(a)(3)94 Stat. 304Pub. L. 99–514, § 2100 Stat. 2095Apr. 2, 1980Oct. 22, 1986“The amendments made by paragraphs (1) and (2) [enacting this section] shall apply to qualified liquidations (within the meaning of section 473(c) of the Internal Revenue Code of 1986 [formerly I.R.C. 1954]) in taxable years ending after .”October 31, 1979

L. 99–514, § 2100 Stat. 2095Apr. 2, 1980Oct. 22, 1986“The amendments made by paragraphs (1) and (2) [enacting this section] shall apply to qualified liquidations (within the meaning of section 473(c) of the Internal Revenue Code of 1986 [formerly I.R.C. 1954]) in taxable years ending after .”October 31, 1979

children.text: there is a qualified liquidation of goods which the taxpayer inventories under the LIFO method, and
the taxpayer elects to have the provisions of this section apply with respect to such liquidation,

children.label: A
children.text: the aggregate replacement cost of the liquidated goods so replaced during such year, over

children.label: B
children.text: the aggregate cost of such goods reflected in the opening inventory of the liquidation year, or

children.label: A
children.text: the aggregate cost reflected in such opening inventory of the liquidated goods so replaced during such year, over

children.label: B
children.text: such aggregate replacement cost.

children.label: A
children.text: a decrease in the closing inventory of the liquidation year from the opening inventory of such year, but only if

children.label: B
children.text: the taxpayer establishes to the satisfaction of the Secretary that such decrease is directly and primarily attributable to a qualified inventory interruption.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: The term “qualified inventory interruption” means a regulation, request, or interruption described in subparagraph (B) but only to the extent provided in the notice published pursuant to subparagraph (B).

children.label: I
children.text: any Department of Energy regulation or request with respect to energy supplies, or

children.label: II
children.text: any embargo, international boycott, or other major foreign trade interruption,

children.label: ii
children.text: that the application of this section to that class of goods and taxpayers is necessary to carry out the purposes of this section,

children.heading: Other definitions and special rules
children.label: d Other definitions and special rules

children.heading: Liquidation year
children.label: 1 Liquidation year
children.text: The term “liquidation year” means the taxable year in which occurs the qualified liquidation to which this section applies.

children.heading: Replacement year
children.label: 2 Replacement year
children.text: The term “replacement year” means any taxable year in the replacement period; except that such term shall not include any taxable year after the taxable year in which replacement of the liquidated goods is completed.

children.label: A
children.text: the period of the 3 taxable years following the liquidation year, or

children.label: B
children.text: the period specified by the Secretary in a notice published in the Federal Register with respect to that qualified inventory interruption.

children.heading: LIFO method
children.label: 4 LIFO method
children.text: The term “LIFO method” means the method of inventorying goods described in section 472.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: An election under subsection (a) shall be made subject to such conditions, and in such manner and form and at such time, as the Secretary may prescribe by regulation.

children.heading: Irrevocable election
children.label: B Irrevocable election
children.text: An election under this section shall be irrevocable and shall be binding for the liquidation year and for all determinations for prior and subsequent taxable years insofar as such determinations are affected by the adjustments under this section.

children.heading: Replacements
children.label: 1 Replacements
children.text: If the closing inventory of the taxpayer for any replacement year reflects an increase over the opening inventory of such goods for such year, the goods reflecting such increase shall be considered, in the order of their acquisition, as having been acquired in replacement of the goods most recently liquidated (whether or not in a qualified liquidation) and not previously replaced.

children.heading: Amount at which replacement goods taken into account
children.label: 2 Amount at which replacement goods taken into account
children.text: In the case of any qualified liquidation, any goods considered under paragraph (1) as having been acquired in replacement of the goods liquidated in such liquidation shall be taken into purchases and included in the closing inventory of the taxpayer for the replacement year at the inventory cost basis of the goods replaced.

children.heading: Special rules for application of adjustments
children.label: f Special rules for application of adjustments
A
children.text: an adjustment is required under this section for any taxable year by reason of the replacement of liquidated goods during any replacement year, and

children.label: B
children.text: the assessment of a deficiency, or the allowance of a credit or refund of an overpayment of tax attributable to such adjustment, for any taxable year, is otherwise prevented by the operation of any law or rule of law (other than section 7122, relating to compromises),

children.heading: Interest
children.label: 2 Interest
children.text: Solely for purposes of determining interest on any overpayment or underpayment attributable to an adjustment made under this section, such overpayment or underpayment shall be treated as an overpayment or underpayment (as the case may be) for the replacement year.

children.heading: Coordination with section 472
children.label: g Coordination with section 472
children.text: The Secretary shall prescribe such regulations as may be necessary to coordinate the provisions of this section with the provisions of section 472.
